items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 17 at 1:53PM AKDT until April 20 at 10:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 25 to 35 mph with gusts up to 60 mph possible.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...From 4 AM Sunday to 10 PM AKDT Monday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Periods of blowing snow may develop where
strong winds and falling snow overlap. Visibilities may be reduced
to 1/2 miles at times, with the highest chances occurring south of
Tr
items.areas: Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 17 at 1:24PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
Wet snow will develop early Sunday morning and continue into the
afternoon. The snow may be heavy at times resulting in wet, slushy
accumulations. By Sunday evening, there is a 40% chance that the
snow transitions to rain or a mix of rain and snow in the
valleys, including Fairbanks. The rain can also be heavy at
times. If there is heavy rain, localized flooding, while not
expected, is possible since the ground is still frozen and any
standing water has nowhere to go. Late Sunday night and Monda
items.areas: White Mountains and High Terrain South of the Yukon River; Upper Chena River Valley; Tanana Flats; Eielson AFB and Salcha; Goldstream Valley and Nenana Hills; Chatanika River Valley; Two Rivers; Fairbanks Metro Area; Nenana; Northern Denali Borough

items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 17 at 1:24PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
Light snow will be around on Saturday but the snow will turn
heavier and steadier through the day on Sunday. Periods of snow
will persist through Monday and into early Tuesday morning before
rapidly dissipating. Snowfall totals over the 3 day period are
expected to be between 5 and 10 inches with the highest amounts
expected over the higher terrain.
items.areas: South Slopes Of The Central Brooks Range; Upper Koyukuk Valley; Central Interior

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 17 at 12:46PM AKDT until April 18 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...For the Winter Weather Advisory. Additional snow
accumulations between 4 and 8 inches. For the Winter Storm Watch,
heavy snow possible. Total snow accumulations between 12 and 18
inches possible. Winds could gust as high as 35 mph.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range South of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...For the Winter Weather Advisory, until 4 PM AKDT Saturday.
For the Winter Storm Watch, from Sunday morning through Monday
afternoon.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult with wet snow on wa
items.areas: Eastern Alaska Range South of Trims Camp
items.instruction:
Monitor the latest forecasts for updates on this situation.

Slow down and use caution while traveling. Visit 511.alaska.gov or
call 5 1 1 for the latest road conditions.

items.headline: Winter Storm Watch issued April 17 at 12:46PM AKDT until April 20 at 7:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and Blowing Snow possible. Total snow accumulations
between 2 and 5 inches possible. Winds may gust as high as 40 mph.

* WHERE...Dalton Highway Summits.

* WHEN...From Sunday morning through Monday morning.

* IMPACTS...Visibilities may drop below 1/4 mile due to falling and
blowing snow. Travel may be impacted.
items.instruction: Monitor the latest forecasts for updates on this situation.

items.headline: Winter Storm Warning issued April 17 at 12:23PM AKDT until April 19 at 10:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow expected. Visibilities may drop below
one quarter mile due to falling and blowing snow. Total snow
accumulations between 3 and 8 inches. Winds gusting as high as 40
to 45 mph.

* WHERE...Kuskokwim Delta Coast and Nunivak Island.

* WHEN...From 10 AM Saturday to 10 AM AKDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest wind gusts will be along the
immediate coastline.
items.areas: Kuskokwim Delta Coast and Nunivak Island
items.instruction:
A Winter Storm Warning for snow means severe winter weather
conditions will make travel very hazardous. If you must travel, keep
an extra flashlight, food and water in your vehicle in case of an
emergency.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 17 at 12:23PM AKDT until April 19 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow expected. Visibilities reduced to one
half mile or less due to blowing snow. Total snow accumulations
between 2 and 9 inches. Winds gusting as high as 40 to 45 mph.

* WHERE...Lower Kuskokwim Valley, and Interior Kuskokwim Delta.

* WHEN...From 4 PM Saturday to 4 PM AKDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult.
items.areas: Kuskokwim Valley West; Kuskokwim Valley East; Lime Village; Interior Kuskokwim Delta
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.

items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 17 at 12:01PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
A front will be moving into the area Saturday evening/night from
the Bering Sea. This front is expected to bring periods of snow,
heavy at times Saturday night through Sunday morning. As the front
progresses north Sunday afternoon, temperatures are expected to
rise into the mid 30s which will support a transition to a
rain/snow mix or plain rain. Prior to the changeover, snowfall
totals will range from 4 to 7 inches. Melting will occur,
especially when the snow mixes or changes to rain Sunday
af
items.areas: Lower Yukon River; Lower Yukon and Innoko Valleys; North Slopes of the Western Alaska Range; Upper Kuskokwim Valley

items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 17 at 12:01PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
As a front moves north on Sunday, areas of snow will move into the
Seward Peninsula. Periods of snow will continue through Sunday
night before lessening in coverage on Monday. Snowfall amounts
will be heaviest east of Nome, especially in the higher terrain.
Snowfall totals will be up to 2 inches in Nome, up to 3 inches in
Buckland/Deering and up to 6 inches in the mountains.
items.areas: Northern Seward Peninsula; Southern Seward Peninsula Coast; Interior Seward Peninsula

items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 17 at 12:01PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
As a front moves north on Sunday, areas of snow will move into the
Kobuk and Koyukuk Valleys. Periods of snow will continue through
Sunday night before lessening in coverage on Monday. Snowfall
totals will be around 3 to 6 inches by Monday night.
items.areas: Upper Kobuk Valleys; Lower Koyukuk Valley

items.headline: Winter Storm Warning issued April 17 at 11:41AM AKDT until April 19 at 10:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow expected. Total snow accumulations
between 3 and 7 inches. Winds gusting as high as 45 mph.

* WHERE...Yukon Delta Coast.

* WHEN...From 10 AM Saturday to 10 AM AKDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Visibilities may drop below 1/4 mile due to falling and
blowing snow. Travel could be very difficult. Areas of blowing
snow could significantly reduce visibility.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest wind gusts will be along the
immediate coastline.
items.instruction:
If you must travel, keep an extra flashlight, food, and water in
your vehicle in case of an emergency.

items.headline: Winter Storm Watch issued April 17 at 11:41AM AKDT until April 19 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow possible. Total snow accumulations
between 3 and 6 inches possible. Winds could gust as high as 45
mph.

* WHERE...Eastern Norton Sound and Nulato Hills.

* WHEN...From late Saturday night through Sunday afternoon.

* IMPACTS...Visibilities may drop below 1/4 mile due to falling and
blowing snow. Travel could be difficult.
items.areas: Eastern Norton Sound and Nulato Hills
items.instruction: Monitor the latest forecasts for updates on this situation.

items.headline: Winter Storm Watch issued April 17 at 11:41AM AKDT until April 20 at 12:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Heavy snow possible. Total snow accumulations between 5 and
10 inches possible.

* WHERE...Middle Yukon Valley.

* WHEN...From late Saturday night through Sunday evening.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Highest totals are expected near Kaltag. With
heavy snow on the rivers, the added weight may result in overflow
and water on top of the ice.
items.instruction: Monitor the latest forecasts for updates on this situation.

items.headline: Winter Storm Watch issued April 17 at 11:41AM AKDT until April 20 at 10:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow possible. Total snow accumulations
around one inch possible. Winds could gust as high as 65 mph.

* WHERE...St Lawrence Island.

* WHEN...From Sunday morning through Monday morning.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted. Visibilities may drop below 1/4
mile due to falling and blowing snow. Very strong winds could
result in power outages.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest wind gusts will occur in Gambell on
Sunday.
items.instruction: Monitor the latest forecasts for updates on this situation.
